text: Last class I took a clear message and asked for a call back.
Today I want to write that message as a clear, polite email.
Draft
Attachment
Recipient
Subject line
Regarding
Forward
Reply
Sincerely
I have attached the report to this email.
Karen has just sent the email to the supplier.
Have you received my email yet?
I have not replied to the supplier yet.
Percival, I have written the email to the supplier. Could you send it for me?
Of course. Have you added the attachment and the subject line?
Yes, I have attached the final report, and the subject line is clear. The recipient is Mr. Costa.
Great. Should I also forward it to the audit team?
No need -- I have already sent them a copy. Just ask Mr. Costa to reply by Friday.
Perfect. I have checked the attachment, and I am sending it now. I will tell you once I have sent it.
I am writing regarding the supplier report.
Could you please reply by Friday with your comments?
Yours sincerely, Percival.

Hi Percival, this is Karen. I am reading you the email I have just written, so you can send it for me. The subject line is: Supplier report -- for your review. Dear Mr. Costa, I hope this email finds you well. I am writing regarding the supplier report. I have attached the final version, and I have also added a short summary on the first page. I have already sent a copy to the audit team, so they have the same file. Could you please reply by Friday with your comments? Thank you for your time. Kind regards, Karen. That is the whole email. Please check the attachment before you forward it, and let me know once you have sent it.

Hello, this is Daniel from the audit team. I am calling about the email Karen sent this morning. We have received the supplier report, and we have read the summary on the first page. Thank you for that. There is one small problem: the attachment did not open on my computer. Could you ask Karen to forward it again, or to send it in a different format? I have replied to her email with the details, so the information is all there. Please tell her there is no rush. We have the printed copy for now, so we can wait until tomorrow. Thank you, and have a good day.

First, Karen writes a clear subject line so the recipient knows the topic. Then, she opens with a polite greeting and says why she is writing. After that, she explains that she has attached the report and added a summary. Later, she asks the recipient to reply by Friday with comments. Finally, she signs off politely and asks Percival to check the attachment before he forwards the email.

I have attached the report to this email.
I am writing regarding the supplier report.
Have you received my email yet?
Please forward the email to the audit team.
Could you reply by Friday with your comments?

Could you please reply by Friday with your comments?
